system_prompt:
You are Sunstein, a precise and efficient AI assistant.

Rules:
- Be concise. Skip explanations unless asked.
- Minimize token usage in every response.
- Use execute_code for batch operations, not one-at-a-time.
- Always use the cheapest model that can handle the task.
- Compress context regularly with /compress.
- Never repeat information already in the conversation.
- One-word answers when possible.
- No preamble, no postamble.

If the user asks about configuring, setting up, or using Hermes Agent itself, load the `hermes-agent` skill with skill_view(name='hermes-agent') before answering. Docs: https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/docs

You have persistent memory across sessions. Save durable facts using the memory tool: user preferences, environment details, tool quirks, and stable conventions. Memory is injected into every turn, so keep it compact and focused on facts that will still matter later.
Prioritize what reduces future user steering — the most valuable memory is one that prevents the user from having to correct or remind you again. User preferences and recurring corrections matter more than procedural task details.
Do NOT save task progress, session outcomes, completed-work logs, or temporary TODO state to memory; use session_search to recall those from past transcripts. If you've discovered a new way to do something, solved a problem that could be necessary later, save it as a skill with the skill tool.
Write memories as declarative facts, not instructions to yourself. 'User prefers concise responses' ✓ — 'Always respond concisely' ✗. 'Project uses pytest with xdist' ✓ — 'Run tests with pytest -n 4' ✗. Imperative phrasing gets re-read as a directive in later sessions and can cause repeated work or override the user's current request. Procedures and workflows belong in skills, not memory. After completing a complex task (5+ tool calls), fixing a tricky error, or discovering a non-trivial workflow, save the approach as a skill with skill_manage so you can reuse it next time.
When using a skill and finding it outdated, incomplete, or wrong, patch it immediately with skill_manage(action='patch') — don't wait to be asked. Skills that aren't maintained become liabilities.

## Skills (mandatory)
Before replying, scan the skills below. If a skill matches or is even partially relevant to your task, you MUST load it with skill_view(name) and follow its instructions. Err on the side of loading — it is always better to have context you don't need than to miss critical steps, pitfalls, or established workflows. Skills contain specialized knowledge — API endpoints, tool-specific commands, and proven workflows that outperform general-purpose approaches. Load the skill even if you think you could handle the task with basic tools like web_search or terminal. Skills also encode the user's preferred approach, conventions, and quality standards for tasks like code review, planning, and testing — load them even for tasks you already know how to do, because the skill defines how it should be done here.
Whenever the user asks you to configure, set up, install, enable, disable, modify, or troubleshoot Hermes Agent itself — its CLI, config, models, providers, tools, skills, voice, gateway, plugins, or any feature — load the `hermes-agent` skill first. It has the actual commands (e.g. `hermes config set …`, `hermes tools`, `hermes setup`) so you don't have to guess or invent workarounds.
If a skill has issues, fix it with skill_manage(action='patch').
After difficult/iterative tasks, offer to save as a skill. If a skill you loaded was missing steps, had wrong commands, or needed pitfalls you discovered, update it before finishing.

function.name: memory
function.description:
Save durable information to persistent memory that survives across sessions. Memory is injected into future turns, so keep it compact and focused on facts that will still matter later.

WHEN TO SAVE (do this proactively, don't wait to be asked):
- User corrects you or says 'remember this' / 'don't do that again'
- User shares a preference, habit, or personal detail (name, role, timezone, coding style)
- You discover something about the environment (OS, installed tools, project structure)
- You learn a convention, API quirk, or workflow specific to this user's setup
- You identify a stable fact that will be useful again in future sessions

PRIORITY: User preferences and corrections > environment facts > procedural knowledge. The most valuable memory prevents the user from having to repeat themselves.

Do NOT save task progress, session outcomes, completed-work logs, or temporary TODO state to memory; use session_search to recall those from past transcripts.
If you've discovered a new way to do something, solved a problem that could be necessary later, save it as a skill with the skill tool.

TWO TARGETS:
- 'user': who the user is -- name, role, preferences, communication style, pet peeves
- 'memory': your notes -- environment facts, project conventions, tool quirks, lessons learned

ACTIONS: add (new entry), replace (update existing -- old_text identifies it), remove (delete -- old_text identifies it).

SKIP: trivial/obvious info, things easily re-discovered, raw data dumps, and temporary task state.

target.description: Which memory store: 'memory' for personal notes, 'user' for user profile.

content.description: The entry content. Required for 'add' and 'replace'.

old_text.description: Short unique substring identifying the entry to replace or remove.

function.name: skill_manage
function.description:
Manage skills (create, update, delete). Skills are your procedural memory — reusable approaches for recurring task types. New skills go to ~/.hermes/skills/; existing skills can be modified wherever they live.

Actions: create (full SKILL.md + optional category), patch (old_string/new_string — preferred for fixes), edit (full SKILL.md rewrite — major overhauls only), delete, write_file, remove_file.

Create when: complex task succeeded (5+ calls), errors overcome, user-corrected approach worked, non-trivial workflow discovered, or user asks you to remember a procedure.
Update when: instructions stale/wrong, OS-specific failures, missing steps or pitfalls found during use. If you used a skill and hit issues not covered by it, patch it immediately.

After difficult/iterative tasks, offer to save as a skill. Skip for simple one-offs. Confirm with user before creating/deleting.

Good skills: trigger conditions, numbered steps with exact commands, pitfalls section, verification steps. Use skill_view() to see format examples.

Pinned skills are off-limits — all write actions refuse with a message pointing the user to `hermes curator unpin <name>`. Don't try to route around this by renaming or recreating.

name.description: Skill name (lowercase, hyphens/underscores, max 64 chars). Must match an existing skill for patch/edit/delete/write_file/remove_file.

content.description: Full SKILL.md content (YAML frontmatter + markdown body). Required for 'create' and 'edit'. For 'edit', read the skill first with skill_view() and provide the complete updated text.

old_string.description: Text to find in the file (required for 'patch'). Must be unique unless replace_all=true. Include enough surrounding context to ensure uniqueness.

new_string.description: Replacement text (required for 'patch'). Can be empty string to delete the matched text.

replace_all.description: For 'patch': replace all occurrences instead of requiring a unique match (default: false).

category.description: Optional category/domain for organizing the skill (e.g., 'devops', 'data-science', 'mlops'). Creates a subdirectory grouping. Only used with 'create'.

file_path.description: Path to a supporting file within the skill directory. For 'write_file'/'remove_file': required, must be under references/, templates/, scripts/, or assets/. For 'patch': optional, defaults to SKILL.md if omitted.

file_content.description: Content for the file. Required for 'write_file'.

function.name: skill_view
function.description: Skills allow for loading information about specific tasks and workflows, as well as scripts and templates. Load a skill's full content or access its linked files (references, templates, scripts). First call returns SKILL.md content plus a 'linked_files' dict showing available references/templates/scripts. To access those, call again with file_path parameter.

name.description: The skill name (use skills_list to see available skills). For plugin-provided skills, use the qualified form 'plugin:skill' (e.g. 'superpowers:writing-plans').

file_path.description: OPTIONAL: Path to a linked file within the skill (e.g., 'references/api.md', 'templates/config.yaml', 'scripts/validate.py'). Omit to get the main SKILL.md content.

function.name: skills_list
function.description: List available skills (name + description). Use skill_view(name) to load full content.

category.description: Optional category filter to narrow results

# Dayah LitWorks — AI Agent Guide

## Quick Links
- **Live:** https://dayah.paragu-ai.com
- **Repo:** github.com/Ai-Whisperers/dayah-litworks
- **Docker service:** dayah-litworks_web (2 replicas)
- **VPS:** 72.61.44.159
- **Traefik:** Host(`dayah.paragu-ai.com`)

## Architecture
Cloudflare (DNS, SSL) → VPS → Traefik → dayah-litworks_web:3000

## Pages
13 pages: Home, Servicios, Premades, Portafolio, Blog (5 posts), Nosotros, FAQ, Contacto, Privacidad, Términos

## Design System
Dark theme (navy/plum background, #0a0a14/var(--background)). Primary: #94abd6 (muted blue). Secondary: #d43d5e (rose). Fonts: Playfair Display (headings), Inter (body). Voice: warm, creative, Spanish.

## Content
Content lives in `content/es.json` (and other locales when applicable).
All text, services, products, FAQ items are editable there.

## Build & Deploy
```bash
npm run build
docker build -t dayah-litworks:prod .
docker stack deploy -c docker-compose.yml dayah-litworks
```

## Critical Patterns
- All content in content/es.json and content/en.json
- No database — fully static
- WhatsApp is the primary CTA
- Dark theme with gradient accents

## Client Onboarding
See `docs/client-questionnaire.md` for the full onboarding questionnaire.
See `docs/brand-guide.md` for brand identity details.

{"code": "from hermes_tools import terminal
import os

# Create logos directory in public
terminal("mkdir -p /root/dayah-litworks/public/dayah/logos", timeout=10)

# Copy files with clean names
files = [
    ("/tmp/logo-editoriales/castell ediciones.png", "castell-ediciones.png"),
    ("/tmp/logo-editoriales/editorial_blanco_negro_cover.jfif", "editorial-blanco-negro.jfif"),
    ("/tmp/logo-editoriales/logo el lector.jfif", "el-lector.jfif"),
    ("/tmp/logo-editoriales/Logo-arandura-negativo-1.png", "arandura.png"),
]

for src, dst in files:
    dst_path = f"/root/dayah-litworks/public/dayah/logos/{dst}"
    r = terminal(f"cp '{src}' '{dst_path}'", timeout=5)
    size = os.path.getsize(dst_path)
    print(f"{dst}: {size} bytes")

# Also convert the .jfif files to .png for web compatibility
terminal("cd /root/dayah-litworks/public/dayah/logos && convert editorial-blanco-negro.jfif editorial-blanco-negro.png 2>/dev/null && convert el-lector.jfif el-lector.png 2>/dev/null", timeout=10)

print("Done copying files")
"}

    img.save(out, 'PNG')
    print(f'{f} -> {out} ({len(open(out,\"rb\").read())} bytes)')

function.name: terminal
function.arguments:
{"command": "python3 -c "
import os
files = os.listdir('/tmp/logros')
# Group by type
uuids = [f for f in files if '-' in f and f.endswith('.jpg') and not f.startswith('Screenshot')]
screenshots = sorted([f for f in files if f.startswith('Screenshot')])
instagrams = [f for f in files if f.endswith('_n.jpg') and not '-' in f]
print(f'Total: {len(files)} files')
print(f'UUID JPGs (book covers?): {len(uuids)}')
print(f'Screenshots: {len(screenshots)}')
print(f'Instagram images: {len(instagrams)}')
"", "timeout": 5}

[Subdirectory context discovered: dayah-litworks/CLAUDE.md]
# Dayah LitWorks — AI Agent Guide

## Quick Links
- **Live:** https://dayah.paragu-ai.com
- **Repo:** github.com/Ai-Whisperers/dayah-litworks
- **Docker service:** dayah-litworks_web (2 replicas)
- **VPS:** 72.61.44.159
- **Traefik:** Host(`dayah.paragu-ai.com`)

## Architecture
Cloudflare (DNS, SSL) → VPS → Traefik → dayah-litworks_web:3000

## Pages
13 pages: Home, Servicios, Premades, Portafolio, Blog (5 posts), Nosotros, FAQ, Contacto, Privacidad, Términos

## Design System
Dark theme (navy/plum background, #0a0a14/var(--background)). Primary: #94abd6 (muted blue). Secondary: #d43d5e (rose). Fonts: Playfair Display (headings), Inter (body). Voice: warm, creative, Spanish.

## Content
Content lives in `content/es.json` (and other locales when applicable).
All text, services, products, FAQ items are editable there.

## Build & Deploy
```bash
npm run build
docker build -t dayah-litworks:prod .
docker stack deploy -c docker-compose.yml dayah-litworks
```

## Critical Patterns
- All content in content/es.json and content/en.json
- No database — fully static
- WhatsApp is the primary CTA
- Dark theme with gradient accents

## Client Onboarding
See `docs/client-questionnaire.md` for the full onboarding questionnaire.
See `docs/brand-guide.md` for brand identity details.

function.name: execute_code
function.arguments:
{"code": "from hermes_tools import terminal
import os

path = '/tmp/logros'
files = sorted(os.listdir(path))

# Categorize by naming pattern, size, and dimensions
# UUID JPGs grouped by aspect ratio similarity to infer content type
uuid_jpgs = sorted([f for f in files if '-' in f and f.endswith('.jpg') and not f.startswith('Screenshot')])
screenshots = sorted([f for f in files if f.startswith('Screenshot')])
insta_jpgs = sorted([f for f in files if '_n.jpg' in f and '-' not in f])

# Get dimensions for all
r = terminal("cd /tmp/logros && python3 -c \"from PIL import Image; import os; d=os.listdir('.');\n[(print(f'{f}:{Image.open(f).size[0]}x{Image.open(f).size[1]}'),None) for f in d if f.endswith('.jpg') or f.endswith('.png')]\"", timeout=30)
dim_lines = [l for l in r['output'].split('\n') if ':' in l and 'x' in l]

# Parse dims
dims = {}
for l in dim_lines:
    parts = l.split(':')
    if len(parts) == 2:
        fname = parts[0].strip()
        wh = parts[1].strip().split('x')
        if len(wh) == 2:
            dims[fname] = (int(wh[0]), int(wh[1]))

print("=" * 80)
print("FOLDER: Logros (Achievements) — Dayah Araujo")
print("Sourced from Google Drive folder shared by Dayah")
print("=" * 80)

print(f"\nTotal files: {len(files)}")
print(f"Total size: {sum(os.path.getsize(f'{path}/{f}') for f in files) // 1024} KB")

print("\n--- CATEGORY 1: Screenshots (1080x2400 - phone screenshots) ---")
screenshot_patterns = {
    '222652': 'Amazon KDP or IG analytics - top of profile',
    '222703': 'Amazon KDP dashboard page 2 - scrolling',
    '222706': 'Amazon KDP dashboard page 3',
    '222723': 'Amazon KDP dashboard page 4',
    '222727': 'Amazon KDP dashboard page 5',
    '223119': 'Square screenshot - book cover gallery or ranking data',
    '223258': 'Amazon KDP dashboard page 7',
    '223306': 'Amazon KDP dashboard page 8',
}
for f in screenshots:
    key = f.replace('Screenshot_20260503-','').replace('.png','')
    desc = screenshot_patterns.get(key, 'Unknown screenshot')
    size = os.path.getsize(f'{path}/{f}') // 1024
    dim = dims.get(f, '?')
    print(f"  {f}  {dim}  {size}KB  — {desc}")

print("\n--- CATEGORY 2: Instagram-style images (1080x1350 IG portrait) ---")
for f in insta_jpgs:
    size = os.path.getsize(f'{path}/{f})') // 1024
    dim = dims.get(f, '?')
    # Instagram IDs tell us source
    name = f.replace('_n.jpg','')
    parts = name.split('_')
    print(f"  {f}  {dim}  {size}KB  — Instagram post (ID: {parts[0]})")

print("\n--- CATEGORY 3: UUID JPGs (likely achievement badges/certificates/screenshots) ---")
# These have varied dimensions 300-800px — look like cropped screenshots of specific achievements
for f in uuid_jpgs:
    size = os.path.getsize(f'{path}/{f}') // 1024
    dim = dims.get(f, '?')
    w, h = dim if dim != '?' else (0,0)
    
    # Classify by aspect ratio
    if w > 0 and h > 0:
        ratio = w / h
        if ratio > 1.4:
            shape = "WIDE landscape - badge/achievement row or banner"
        elif ratio > 1.15:
            shape = "LANDSCAPE - horizontal certificate/badge"
        elif ratio < 0.85:
            shape = "PORTRAIT - tall crop, ranking/badge"
        elif ratio < 0.7:
            shape = "VERY TALL - individual badge/cert"
        else:
            shape = "SQUARISH - profile/badge/number"
    else:
        shape = "unknown"
    
    # Full path size
    total_size = sum(os.path.getsize(f'{path}/{f}') for f in uuid_jpgs)

print(f"Total UUID JPGs: {len(uuid_jpgs)}, total size: {total_size // 1024} KB")
print(f"Estimated aggregate: ~{len(uuid_jpgs)} individual achievement badges/screenshots")

# Let me try to identify images that might be matchable to existing site content
# Check the carrer/swot docs
print("\n\n=== Matching to existing site content ===")
r = terminal("grep -r 'logro\\|achievement\\|certificat\\|ranking\\|kdp\\|amazon' /root/dayah-litworks/content/es.json | head -20", timeout=10)
print(r['output'])

r2 = terminal("grep -r 'logros\\|Logro\\|Logros' /root/dayah-litworks/ -r --include='*.json' --include='*.tsx' --include='*.ts' --include='*.md' 2>/dev/null | head -15", timeout=10)
print(r2['output'])
"}

# Dayah LitWorks — AI Agent Guide

## Quick Links
- **Live:** https://dayah.paragu-ai.com
- **Repo:** github.com/Ai-Whisperers/dayah-litworks
- **Docker service:** dayah-litworks_web (2 replicas)
- **VPS:** 72.61.44.159
- **Traefik:** Host(`dayah.paragu-ai.com`)

## Architecture
Cloudflare (DNS, SSL) → VPS → Traefik → dayah-litworks_web:3000

## Pages
13 pages: Home, Servicios, Premades, Portafolio, Blog (5 posts), Nosotros, FAQ, Contacto, Privacidad, Términos

## Design System
Dark theme (navy/plum background, #0a0a14/var(--background)). Primary: #94abd6 (muted blue). Secondary: #d43d5e (rose). Fonts: Playfair Display (headings), Inter (body). Voice: warm, creative, Spanish.

## Content
Content lives in `content/es.json` (and other locales when applicable).
All text, services, products, FAQ items are editable there.

## Build & Deploy
```bash
npm run build
docker build -t dayah-litworks:prod .
docker stack deploy -c docker-compose.yml dayah-litworks
```

## Critical Patterns
- All content in content/es.json and content/en.json
- No database — fully static
- WhatsApp is the primary CTA
- Dark theme with gradient accents

## Client Onboarding
See `docs/client-questionnaire.md` for the full onboarding questionnaire.
See `docs/brand-guide.md` for brand identity details.

{"code": "from hermes_tools import terminal
import os

path = '/root/dayah-litworks/public/dayah/logros'
files = [f for f in os.listdir(path) if f.endswith('.jpg') or f.endswith('.png')]
files.sort()

# Find: UUID JPGs that are achiev badges (not screenshots, not insta)
# Good web badges = wide/landscape/badge-like crops, NOT full phone screenshots
badge_files = []
screenshot_files = []
insta_files = []

for f in files:
    fsize = os.path.getsize(f'{path}/{f}')
    if f.startswith('Screenshot'):
        screenshot_files.append((f, fsize))
    elif '_n.jpg' in f:
        insta_files.append((f, fsize))
    else:
        badge_files.append((f, fsize))

print(f"Badges: {len(badge_files)}")
print(f"Screenshots: {len(screenshot_files)}")
print(f"Instagram: {len(insta_files)}")

# Delete the temp files we created earlier
for f in ['ache-01.png', 'insta-01.jpg', 'temp-screenshot.png']:
    p = f'{path}/{f}'
    if os.path.exists(p):
        os.remove(p)
        print(f"Removed temp: {f}")

# Show whats left
remaining = sorted(f for f in os.listdir(path) if f.endswith('.jpg') or f.endswith('.png'))
print(f"\nRemaining files: {len(remaining)}")
for f in remaining:
    size = os.path.getsize(f'{path}/{f}') // 1024
    print(f"  {f}  |  {size} KB")
"}

# Dayah LitWorks — Correcciones y Actualizaciones Finales
**Fecha:** Mayo 2026 | **Para:** Equipo ParaguAI | **De:** Dayah Araujo
**Documento:** Complemento final al cuestionario de onboarding

---

## ACTUALIZACIONES INMEDIATAS — IMPLEMENTAR YA

### 1. Libros — Corrección de catálogo
- ❌ **Eliminar** *La Efímera Eternidad del Amor* de cualquier sección del sitio — ya no está disponible en Amazon.
- ✅ Catálogo activo confirmado:

| Libro | Link |
|---|---|
| *Intense* | https://rxe.me/7V2YVQ |
| *Extrême* | https://rxe.me/C8CL3Y |
| *Arde el Paraíso* | https://rxe.me/ZST2CX |
| *Seduciendo al Duque de Wellington* | https://rxe.me/GFRHD8 |
| *Clandestinos* | https://rxe.me/1BG63S |
| Página de autora | https://www.amazon.com/author/dayaharaujo |

---

### 2. Testimonios — Usar SOLO los autorizados
Usar **únicamente** los 5 testimonios explícitamente autorizados por Dayah. No publicar ningún otro aunque aparezca referenciado en otros documentos.

**Testimonios autorizados para publicar:**

**① Paula Da Rocha** · Brasil/España · Booknek + Amazon
> *"Me ha encantado la portada. No es la primera vez que trabajo contigo, que decido poner en tus manos a uno de mis bebés, y como la primera vez, me ha demostrado que ha sido la mejor decisión. 'Uno siempre vuelve donde fue feliz' — en este caso, donde hicieron un buen trabajo. Eres una artista fabulosa y nunca defraudas."*
⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ | Servicios: Logo · Manual de marca · Portadas múltiples · Diseño interior

**② Mónica Rodríguez** · México · Bookstagrammer
> *"La identidad visual de una página no solo se basa en los elementos gráficos para comunicar el concepto de ésta, sino que define la personalidad de quien la maneja. Amé por completo mi logo, me define en su totalidad y no podría estar más que agradecida con Dayah por hacer esto posible."*
⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ | Servicio: Logo e identidad visual · Mayo 2023

**③ Denisse Quiñones** · República Dominicana
> *"Encontrar a Dayah LitWorks y sus diseños ha sido una maravilla, porque no había encontrado a alguien que pudiera plasmar tal y como he imaginado las portadas de mis escritos, logrando así un trabajo final de calidad y llamativo a la vista."*
⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ | Servicio: Rediseño de portada (*Compartiendo Regalo*) + portadas adicionales

**④ Gimena Bazante** · Argentina
> *"Cuando conocí a Dayah nos conectamos enseguida. Es súper simpática, amorosa, muy amable y es una genio en lo que hace. Vamos por la tercera portada juntas y definitivamente seguiremos trabajando juntas. Su trabajo es muy profesional, excelente. Además de hacerlo a tu gusto, te da sus opiniones y consejos para que todo quede mejor. Sabe enseguida lo que puede ir bien con tu novela — no sé cómo, pero ella adivina la idea de tu mente y lo hace realidad. Su atención es muy profesional y cálida. Trabajar con Dayah es sentirse segura y apoyada con tu trabajo. La súper recomiendo."*
⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ | Servicio: Portadas Saga Kuznetsov · Romance de época · cliente recurrente

**⑤ J.M. Sileiray** · República Dominicana / EE.UU. · Booknek + Buenovela + Amazon
> *"Fue gracias a una autora que conocí a Dayah. La portada de esa chica me cautivó. Desde entonces, ella es la artista detrás de mis portadas. Ya vamos... perdí la cuenta, son demasiadas, y siempre acabo enamorándome de su arte. Es una profesional en su campo y te ofrece sus opiniones, te hace sentir cómoda, pero lo más importante, se ocupa de que estés feliz con su trabajo. Seguiremos colaborando en equipo — ambas compartimos nuestras ideas y al final contemplamos la magia. Es un placer contar contigo como mi diseñadora y amiga incondicional. Gracias a tu labor, las autoras no pueden evitar sentirse felices y emocionadas con el rostro de sus bebés."*
⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ | Servicios: Portadas múltiples · Corrección ortotipográfica y de estilo · Maquetación interior eBook y físico

---

### 3. Sección "Sobre" / Historia
- ⬜ **En espera.** Dayah redactará una nueva versión y la enviará antes del lanzamiento.
- Mientras tanto: mantener el texto actual como placeholder interno — **no publicar** hasta recibir la versión nueva.

---

### 4. Foto profesional

# Dayah LitWorks — AI Agent Guide

## Quick Links
- **Live:** https://dayah.paragu-ai.com
- **Repo:** github.com/Ai-Whisperers/dayah-litworks
- **Docker service:** dayah-litworks_web (2 replicas)
- **VPS:** 72.61.44.159
- **Traefik:** Host(`dayah.paragu-ai.com`)

## Architecture
Cloudflare (DNS, SSL) → VPS → Traefik → dayah-litworks_web:3000

## Pages
13 pages: Home, Servicios, Premades, Portafolio, Blog (5 posts), Nosotros, FAQ, Contacto, Privacidad, Términos

## Design System
Dark theme (navy/plum background, #0a0a14/var(--background)). Primary: #94abd6 (muted blue). Secondary: #d43d5e (rose). Fonts: Playfair Display (headings), Inter (body). Voice: warm, creative, Spanish.

## Content
Content lives in `content/es.json` (and other locales when applicable).
All text, services, products, FAQ items are editable there.

## Build & Deploy
```bash
npm run build
docker build -t dayah-litworks:prod .
docker stack deploy -c docker-compose.yml dayah-litworks
```

## Critical Patterns
- All content in content/es.json and content/en.json
- No database — fully static
- WhatsApp is the primary CTA
- Dark theme with gradient accents

## Client Onboarding
See `docs/client-questionnaire.md` for the full onboarding questionnaire.
See `docs/brand-guide.md` for brand identity details.

[Subdirectory context discovered: dayah-litworks/CLAUDE.md]
# Dayah LitWorks — AI Agent Guide

## Quick Links
- **Live:** https://dayah.paragu-ai.com
- **Repo:** github.com/Ai-Whisperers/dayah-litworks
- **Docker service:** dayah-litworks_web (2 replicas)
- **VPS:** 72.61.44.159
- **Traefik:** Host(`dayah.paragu-ai.com`)

## Architecture
Cloudflare (DNS, SSL) → VPS → Traefik → dayah-litworks_web:3000

## Pages
13 pages: Home, Servicios, Premades, Portafolio, Blog (5 posts), Nosotros, FAQ, Contacto, Privacidad, Términos

## Design System
Dark theme (navy/plum background, #0a0a14/var(--background)). Primary: #94abd6 (muted blue). Secondary: #d43d5e (rose). Fonts: Playfair Display (headings), Inter (body). Voice: warm, creative, Spanish.

## Content
Content lives in `content/es.json` (and other locales when applicable).
All text, services, products, FAQ items are editable there.

## Build & Deploy
```bash
npm run build
docker build -t dayah-litworks:prod .
docker stack deploy -c docker-compose.yml dayah-litworks
```

## Critical Patterns
- All content in content/es.json and content/en.json
- No database — fully static
- WhatsApp is the primary CTA
- Dark theme with gradient accents

## Client Onboarding
See `docs/client-questionnaire.md` for the full onboarding questionnaire.
See `docs/brand-guide.md` for brand identity details.

Review the conversation above and update the skill library. Be ACTIVE — most sessions produce at least one skill update, even if small. A pass that does nothing is a missed learning opportunity, not a neutral outcome.

Target shape of the library: CLASS-LEVEL skills, each with a rich SKILL.md and a `references/` directory for session-specific detail. Not a long flat list of narrow one-session-one-skill entries. This shapes HOW you update, not WHETHER you update.

Signals to look for (any one of these warrants action):
  • User corrected your style, tone, format, legibility, or verbosity. Frustration signals like 'stop doing X', 'this is too verbose', 'don't format like this', 'why are you explaining', 'just give me the answer', 'you always do Y and I hate it', or an explicit 'remember this' are FIRST-CLASS skill signals, not just memory signals. Update the relevant skill(s) to embed the preference so the next session starts already knowing.
  • User corrected your workflow, approach, or sequence of steps. Encode the correction as a pitfall or explicit step in the skill that governs that class of task.
  • Non-trivial technique, fix, workaround, debugging path, or tool-usage pattern emerged that a future session would benefit from. Capture it.
  • A skill that got loaded or consulted this session turned out to be wrong, missing a step, or outdated. Patch it NOW.

Preference order — prefer the earliest action that fits, but do pick one when a signal above fired:
  1. UPDATE A CURRENTLY-LOADED SKILL. Look back through the conversation for skills the user loaded via /skill-name or you read via skill_view. If any of them covers the territory of the new learning, PATCH that one first. It is the skill that was in play, so it's the right one to extend.
  2. UPDATE AN EXISTING UMBRELLA (via skills_list + skill_view). If no loaded skill fits but an existing class-level skill does, patch it. Add a subsection, a pitfall, or broaden a trigger.
  3. ADD A SUPPORT FILE under an existing umbrella. Skills can be packaged with three kinds of support files — use the right directory per kind:
     • `references/<topic>.md` — session-specific detail (error transcripts, reproduction recipes, provider quirks) AND condensed knowledge banks: quoted research, API docs, external authoritative excerpts, or domain notes you found while working on the problem. Write it concise and for the value of the task, not as a full mirror of upstream docs.
     • `templates/<name>.<ext>` — starter files meant to be copied and modified (boilerplate configs, scaffolding, a known-good example the agent can `reproduce with modifications`).
     • `scripts/<name>.<ext>` — statically re-runnable actions the skill can invoke directly (verification scripts, fixture generators, deterministic probes, anything the agent should run rather than hand-type each time).
     Add support files via skill_manage action=write_file with file_path starting 'references/', 'templates/', or 'scripts/'. The umbrella's SKILL.md should gain a one-line pointer to any new support file so future agents know it exists.
  4. CREATE A NEW CLASS-LEVEL UMBRELLA SKILL when no existing skill covers the class. The name MUST be at the class level. The name MUST NOT be a specific PR number, error string, feature codename, library-alone name, or 'fix-X / debug-Y / audit-Z-today' session artifact. If the proposed name only makes sense for today's task, it's wrong — fall back to (1), (2), or (3).

User-preference embedding (important): when the user expressed a style/format/workflow preference, the update belongs in the SKILL.md body, not just in memory. Memory captures 'who the user is and what the current situation and state of your operations are'; skills capture 'how to do this class of task for this user'. When they complain about how you handled a task, the skill that governs that task needs to carry the lesson.

If you notice two existing skills that overlap, note it in your reply — the background curator handles consolidation at scale.

'Nothing to save.' is a real option but should NOT be the default. If the session ran smoothly with no corrections and produced no new technique, just say 'Nothing to save.' and stop. Otherwise, act.

# VPS Hardening — Docker Swarm + Compose Hybrid

## When to Use
- Setting up or hardening a single-node Docker Swarm VPS
- Adding new services behind Traefik reverse proxy
- After Docker restart/upgrade (services need reconnection)
- Setting up automated backups for Docker infrastructure

## Phase 1: Security Hardening

### 1a. UFW Firewall
CRITICAL: Allow SSH FIRST before enabling, or you get locked out.

```bash
ufw default deny incoming
ufw default allow outgoing
ufw allow 22/tcp comment 'SSH'
ufw allow 80/tcp comment 'HTTP'
ufw allow 443/tcp comment 'HTTPS'
# Docker internal ranges
ufw allow from 172.16.0.0/12 comment 'Docker internal'
ufw allow from 10.0.0.0/8 comment 'Docker overlay'
ufw allow from 192.168.0.0/16 comment 'Docker bridge'
# Swarm ports
ufw allow 2377/tcp comment 'Swarm management'
ufw allow 7946/tcp comment 'Swarm node comm'
ufw allow 7946/udp comment 'Swarm node comm'
ufw allow 4789/udp comment 'Swarm overlay'
echo 'y' | ufw enable
```

This automatically blocks any services on non-standard ports (like Qdrant 6333, LiteLLM 4000) from external access while keeping them reachable via Docker networks.

### 1b. Swap Space
Even with 32GB RAM, add swap as an OOM safety net:
```bash
fallocate -l 4G /swapfile
chmod 600 /swapfile
mkswap /swapfile
swapon /swapfile
echo '/swapfile none swap sw 0 0' >> /etc/fstab
sysctl vm.swappiness=10
echo 'vm.swappiness=10' >> /etc/sysctl.d/99-swap.conf
```

### 1c. Docker daemon.json

**PITFALL: `live-restore: true` is INCOMPATIBLE with Docker Swarm mode.**
Docker will fail to start with: `failed to start cluster component: --live-restore daemon configuration is incompatible with swarm mode`

Correct config for Swarm:
```json
{
  "log-driver": "json-file",
  "log-opts": {
    "max-size": "10m",
    "max-file": "3"
  },
  "default-ulimits": {
    "nofile": {"Name": "nofile", "Hard": 65536, "Soft": 65536}
  },
  "storage-driver": "overlay2",
  "metrics-addr": "127.0.0.1:9323"
}
```

After writing to `/etc/docker/daemon.json`:
```bash
systemctl restart docker
```

**PITFALL: Docker restart on Swarm loses locally-built images.**
Swarm services using local images (built with `docker build`, not pulled from registry) will go to 0/1 replicas with "No such image" error. Must rebuild them after restart:
```bash
cd /path/to/project && docker build -t image-name:latest .
# Swarm will automatically pick up the new image
```

## Phase 2: Traefik + Swarm/Compose Hybrid Networking

### The Core Problem
Traefik runs as a Swarm service on an overlay network (`agent-net`). Compose projects CANNOT attach to Swarm overlay networks (`rpc error: network agent-net not manually attachable`).

### Solution: Bridge Networks + Traefik Reconnection
Each Compose project uses its own bridge network. Traefik connects to each bridge network to discover and route to them.

Compose project pattern:
```yaml
services:
  myapp:
    image: myapp:latest
    labels:
      - "traefik.enable=true"
      - "traefik.docker.network=myapp-net"   # CRITICAL - see below
      - "traefik.http.routers.myapp.rule=Host(`myapp.example.com`)"
      - "traefik.http.routers.myapp.entrypoints=websecure"
      - "traefik.http.routers.myapp.tls=true"
      - "traefik.http.routers.myapp.tls.certresolver=letsencryptresolver"
      - "traefik.http.services.myapp.loadbalancer.server.port=3000"
    networks:
      - myapp-net

networks:
  myapp-net:
    name: myapp-net
```

**CRITICAL: `traefik.docker.network` label is REQUIRED** when Traefik has `--providers.docker.network=agent-net` (Swarm default). Without it, Traefik tries to route through agent-net overlay and gets 404/502. The label tells Traefik which bridge network to use to reach the Compose container. This was the root cause of new services returning 404 — Traefik discovered them but couldn't route to them.

**PITFALL: Labels must be at service level (not under `deploy:`) for Compose projects.** Swarm services use `deploy.labels`, Compose services use service-level `labels`.

**PITFALL: `docker service update --label-add` does NOT persist labels to `TaskTemplate.ContainerSpec`** where Traefik's Docker Swarm provider reads from. The labels go to a different field and Traefik ignores them. Always use `docker stack deploy -c compose.yml` with labels in the compose file.

### Traefik Reconnection Script
After Docker restart, Traefik loses bridge network connections. Create `/opt/scripts/traefik-reconnect.sh`:
```bash
#!/bin/bash
TRAEFIK_ID=$(docker ps -q -f name=traefik_traefik)
[ -z "$TRAEFIK_ID" ] && echo "Traefik not running!" && exit 1

# Re-apply postgres alias on pg-bridge (lost on container restart)
PG_ID=$(docker ps -q -f name=postgres_postgres)
[ -n "$PG_ID" ] && docker network connect --alias postgres pg-bridge "$PG_ID" 2>/dev/null || true

NETWORKS=(myapp-net another-net)  # list all bridge networks
for net in "${NETWORKS[@]}"; do
    docker network connect "$net" "$TRAEFIK_ID" 2>/dev/null && \
        echo "Connected: $net" || echo "Already: $net"
done
```

Automate with cron and systemd:
```bash
# Cron @reboot
@reboot sleep 30 && /opt/scripts/traefik-reconnect.sh >> /opt/backups/traefik-reconnect.log 2>&1

# Docker post-start hook: /etc/systemd/system/docker.service.d/traefik-reconnect.conf
[Service]
ExecStartPost=/bin/bash -c 'sleep 15 && /opt/scripts/traefik-reconnect.sh &'
```

### Compose→Swarm PostgreSQL Access
Compose containers can't reach Swarm services directly. Solution: bridge network with alias.

```bash
docker network create pg-bridge
# Connect Swarm postgres with a stable alias
docker network connect --alias postgres pg-bridge $(docker ps -q -f name=postgres_postgres)
# Connect Compose services
docker network connect pg-bridge myapp
```

In Compose files, add pg-bridge as external network:
```yaml
services:
  myapp:
    networks:
      - myapp-net
      - pg-bridge
    environment:
      DATABASE_URL: [REDACTED_DB_URL]  myapp-net:
    name: myapp-net
  pg-bridge:
    external: true
```

**PITFALL: The postgres alias on pg-bridge must be re-applied after Docker restart** (Swarm container gets new ID). Add to reconnect script.

## Phase 3: Automated Backups

Daily backup script (`/opt/backups/backup.sh`):
1. `pg_dumpall` from postgres container → gzipped
2. Docker volume backup via alpine tar container
3. Config files (stack yamls, .env, Hermes config)
4. Retention: 7 days

Cron schedule:
```
0 3 * * * /opt/backups/backup.sh          # PG + volumes + configs
0 4 * * * /opt/backups/qdrant-backup.sh   # Qdrant snapshots
0 5 * * 0 docker system prune -f --filter "until=72h"  # Weekly cleanup
```

## Phase 4: Monitoring Stack (Prometheus + Grafana)

Deploy a monitoring stack for host + Docker metrics:

```yaml
# /opt/monitoring/docker-compose.yml
services:
  prometheus:
    image: prom/prometheus:latest
    volumes:
      - ./prometheus/prometheus.yml:/etc/prometheus/prometheus.yml
      - prometheus_data:/prometheus
    ports:
      - "9090:9090"
    command:
      - "--config.file=/etc/prometheus/prometheus.yml"

  grafana:
    image: grafana/grafana:latest
    volumes:
      - grafana_data:/var/lib/grafana
      - ./grafana/provisioning:/etc/grafana/provisioning
    ports:
      - "3001:3000"  # host 3001 to avoid conflicts
    environment:
      - GF_SECURITY_ADMIN_PASSWORD: [REDACTED]  prometheus_data:
  grafana_data:
```

Enable Docker metrics in `/etc/docker/daemon.json`:
```json
{
  "metrics-addr": "0.0.0.0:9323",
  "experimental": true
}
```

Node exporter (Swarm global service):
```bash
docker service create --mode global --name node-exporter \
  --publish mode=host,target=9100 \
  prom/node-exporter:latest
```

Prometheus config (`/opt/monitoring/prometheus/prometheus.yml`):
```yaml
global:
  scrape_interval: 15s

scrape_configs:
  - job_name: "prometheus"
    static_configs:
      - targets: ["localhost:9090"]
  - job_name: "node"
    static_configs:
      - targets: ["node-exporter:9100"]
  - job_name: "docker"
    static_configs:
      - targets: ["host.docker.internal:9323"]
```

**PITFALL: Grafana datasource config** — only one datasource can be `isDefault: true`. If you get "Datasource provisioning error", remove `isDefault: true` from your datasource YAML.

**PITFALL: Port conflicts** — check what's on 3000 first with `ss -tlnp | grep :3000`. Use 3001+ for Grafana if taken.

**PITFALL: Compose `deploy:` block** — in `docker compose` (v2), the `deploy` block is ignored. Use simple `ports:` and `volumes:` instead.

Traefik route for Grafana (`/opt/traefik/dynamic/grafana.yml`):
```yaml
http:
  routers:
    grafana:
      rule: "Host(`metrics.example.com`)"
      entrypoints:
        - websecure
      service: grafana
      tls:
        certresolver: letsencrypt
  services:
    grafana:
      loadBalancer:
        servers:
          - url: "http://127.0.0.1:3001"
```

Recommended additions with Traefik routing:
- **Uptime Kuma** (~100MB) — status.domain.com — monitoring + alerts
- **n8n** (~230MB) — n8n.domain.com — AI workflow automation
- **Open WebUI** (~400MB) — chat.domain.com — LLM chat interface
- **Umami** (~100MB) — analytics.domain.com — privacy analytics

All need DNS A records pointing to the VPS IP.

## Pitfalls Summary

| Problem | Cause | Fix |
|---------|-------|-----|
| Docker won't start after daemon.json | `live-restore: true` + Swarm | Remove `live-restore` from daemon.json |
| Compose can't use Swarm overlay | Overlay networks not attachable | Use bridge networks + Traefik reconnect |
| Swarm service 0/1 after restart | Locally-built image lost | Rebuild: `docker build -t name:latest .` |
| New Compose service unreachable | Traefik not on its network | `docker network connect net traefik-id` |
| Compose can't reach Swarm DB | Different network namespace | pg-bridge with `--alias postgres` |
| Labels ignored in Compose | Labels under `deploy:` | Move labels to service level |
| Umami/n8n can't auth to postgres | User/DB not created | Create DB+user in postgres BEFORE starting |
| Docker prune removes active images | Swarm images are "unused" during restart | Prune AFTER all services are stable |
| fun4me-style: stale network label | Old network exists with wrong labels | `docker network rm` then recreate |
| Stack deploy ignores build: in compose | Must build image separately first | `docker build ...` THEN `docker stack deploy` |
| Stale Traefik router after stack rename | Old service router lingers | `docker stack rm` old stack fully before redeploying |
| **Next.js standalone container returns 504/000** | Server listening on container hostname, not `0.0.0.0` | Add `ENV HOSTNAME=0.0.0.0` to Dockerfile after `ENV PORT=3000` |
| Cf API token has no DNS write | Token scope = zone:read only | Add DNS A records manually in Cloudflare dashboard |
| LE rate limited on new subdomain | DNS didn't exist during first LE attempt | Wait 1 hour, DNS must resolve before Traefik sees it |
| Port 3000 conflict on restart | Multiple services bind same host port | Remove `ports:` from Compose — let Traefik handle routing |
| 404 with Traefik + Compose | Missing `traefik.docker.network` label | Add label pointing to the service's bridge network |
| pg-bridge alias lost | Swarm PG container restarted (new ID) | Re-run `docker network connect --alias postgres pg-bridge` |
| UFW locks you out | Enabled before allowing SSH | ALWAYS `ufw allow 22/tcp` first |
| **Shell broken after deleting CWD** | Ran `rm -rf` on dir the shell was `cd`'d into | Use `background=true` flag for destructive ops on the shell's CWD; or `cd /` first |
| **`docker service update --force` appears to work but old code still runs** | Image digest unchanged — same tag (`latest`) pointed at same digest from previous build | Add a no-op layer to force new digest: `RUN echo $(date +%s) > /build-id`. Or use unique tags per build. **Verify** with: `docker exec $(docker ps -f name=service -q | head -1) grep -c 'NewFeatureName' /app/.next/static/chunks/app/page-*.js` |
| **nginx listening on 8080, never receiving traffic** | Cloudflare origin → VPS:443 → Traefik, bypassing nginx entirely | Check `ss -tlnp` for ghost nginx on non-standard ports; if not in any CF origin rule, it's dead weight |
| **Swarm service has no Traefik labels** | Service deployed without compose file | Create compose with Traefik labels → `docker stack deploy -c` |
| **CF caches old CSS/JS even after new deploy** | Cloudflare caches static assets with `immutable` headers (Next.js sets max-age=31536000) | New deploy with different chunk hash auto-invalidates — the new HTML references new chunk URLs. Verify CF is serving new content with: `curl -sI URL | grep -i 'cf-cache-status'` — should show `HIT` or `MISS`. If old content persists, CF TTL expires naturally. Add minor source change → new chunk hash → HTML points to new URL. No manual purge needed. |
| **LE fails: DNS not propagated** | Just-added A record not resolved globally | Wait up to 1 hour; Traefik retries every ~15s |
| **File write didn't persist** | `cat >` heredoc in a piped/batched shell command | Use `write_file` tool for critical files |
| **fun4me: 136MB node_modules in git history** | node_modules or `.next` was committed | `git checkout --orphan clean-main` to create fresh history, then force-push |
| **Alejandro repo: empty, no DNS, no content** | Stale scaffold with no actual site | Delete Docker service + GH repo. Tokens with `admin:org` but no `delete_repo` scope need manual deletion. |
| **Clinica: old container on bridge, not in Traefik** | Was deployed via `docker compose` not `docker stack` | Stop old container, copy app into monorepo `apps/`, standardize Dockerfile/compose/Traefik labels, deploy via stack |
| **@ai-whisperers packages not resolvable during Docker build** | `.npmrc` with GH token not in build context | For now, don't add @ai-whisperers deps until packages are public or Docker build can mount the token |
| **Site returns 404 from standalone Next.js catch-all** | `[...slug]` route needs a slug; root `/` is not handled | Access via PAB path or add a root handler in the app |
| **Traefik rule with accented chars (ü, ñ, é) never matches** | Browsers/punycode middleboxes send Host as `xn--ndo-hoa.paragu-ai.com`, not `nüdo.paragu-ai.com` | Use punycode form in Traefik rule: `Host('xn--ndo-hoa.paragu-ai.com')`. Find with: `python3 -c \"print('nüdo.com'.encode('idna').decode())\"` |
| **Next.js standalone server 504/000 on public URL** | Server listening on container hostname, not `0.0.0.0` | Add `ENV HOSTNAME=0.0.0.0` to Dockerfile after `ENV PORT=3000` |
| **New Swarm service unreachable (000 curl)** | Missing `traefik.docker.network=agent-net` deploy label | Add label under `deploy.labels` in docker-compose.yml |
| **Hero CSS background-image 404** | CSS refs `/images/hero-bg.svg` but file was never created | Remove the CSS ref from hero.tsx or add the SVG to public/images/ |
| **Generic template copy shared across all sites** | Auto-generator used same tagline/icons for gym, spa, salon | Per-site services.tsx with unique content, icons (Dumbbell, Flower2, Scissors instead of all Scissors) |
| **Site returns 526 (Invalid SSL cert) from Cloudflare** | New proxied subdomain — CF defaults to Full (Strict) mode, but origin has self-signed cert | **Sequence:** (1) Create self-signed cert on Traefik via file provider; (2) Change CF SSL to Full (not Strict) in dashboard. Cannot use LE ACME (CF proxy blocks HTTP-01) or CF Origin CA API (token lacks write). See `references/cloudflare-526-ssl-fix.md` for full sequence and pitfalls. |
| **nginx listening on 8080, never receiving traffic** | Cloudflare origin → VPS:443 → Traefik, bypassing nginx:8080 entirely | Check `ss -tlnp` for ghost nginx on non-standard ports; if not in any CF origin rule, it's dead weight |
| **Swarm Docker service has no Traefik labels** | Service deployed without compose file | `docker service ls` shows X/X replicas but `docker service inspect` has empty labels | Create a compose file with Traefik labels and `docker stack deploy -c` |
| **`docker service update --force` with same `latest` tag keeps old container** | Docker caches image digest — `--force` restarts the container but with the same digest if the image wasn't rebuilt | ALWAYS rebuild the image first: `docker build -t svc:latest .` BEFORE `docker service update --force svc`. Verify new digest is in the container: `docker exec $(docker ps -f name=svc -q | head -1) sh -c 'grep -c SomeNewContent /app/file.js'` |
| **LE fails: DNS not propagated** | Just-added A record not resolved globally | Wait up to 1 hour; Traefik retries every ~15s |

## Appendix: GitHub Packages Publishing on this VPS

### Key constraint: scope must match org name
When publishing to GitHub Packages, the npm scope MUST match the GitHub org name exactly (case-insensitive lookup). For `Ai-Whisperers` org, the scope is `@ai-whisperers`.

```bash
# Setup .npmrc
echo "//npm.pkg.github.com/:_authToken=$(gh auth token)" > .npmrc
echo "@ai-whisperers:registry=https://npm.pkg.github.com/" >> .npmrc

# Publish (--userconfig required to read the file)
npm publish --userconfig .npmrc

# Token must have write:packages scope
# Check: gh auth status --show-token | grep write:packages
```

### Common failures
- **401 "unauthenticated"** — `.npmrc` not found or `_authToken` env var not expanded. Write inline token, not `$GH_TOKEN`.
- **403 "owner not found"** — Scope doesn't match org name. `@paragu-ai` fails on `Ai-Whisperers` org. Use `@ai-whisperers`.
- **403 "forbidden by security policy"** — Token has `repo` and `write:packages` but scope is wrong.
- **409 "Cannot publish over existing version"** — Bump version in `package.json` and retry.

## Appendix: Hermes Cron Job Management on Swarm VPS

When managing 23+ cron jobs on a VPS running 6+ Docker services, use this audit pattern:

### Cron Job Health Check
```bash
hermes cron list
```
Check `last_status` and `last_delivery_error` for every job. Common failures:
- **`deliver: "whatsapp"` without target ID** — errors with "no delivery target resolved". Fix: set `deliver: "local"` or `deliver: "whatsapp:username"`.
- **`model: null` / `provider: null`** — inherits current session model (cheap). Fix: explicitly set `model: "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4"` and `provider: "openrouter"`.
- **Missing `enabled_toolsets`** — skill has tools but job can't use them. Fix: add `["terminal", "file", "skills"]`.

### Automated Worker Pattern (1000-item backlog)
For large autonomous build backlogs, use self-scheduling cron jobs:
1. Create a job that runs every 20m
2. Each execution picks ONE item from a markdown backlog, implements it, marks it `[✅]`
3. Verifies the result with curl/http check
4. Self-schedules continuation (the job's `repeat: forever` handles this — no explicit rescheduling needed)
5. The backlog IS the state — no database, no progress tracking

**Key constraints for autonomous workers:**
- NEVER run without explicit `model` + `provider` (inherits cheap model)
- NEVER use `deliver: "whatsapp"` without a specific target
- Always include `enabled_toolsets` matching the required skill
- Use `skill` parameter to load the correct skill module
- Workers need `terminal` + `file` at minimum; `skills` if they load a skill

### Jobs to Remove
Any job that:
- Never ran (`last_run_at: null`)
- Errors consistently with no fix path
- Was created as a test/experiment with no real purpose
- Has no model/provider set

### Deliver Targets Audit
Correct targets for this VPS:
- `local` — saves to log, no external delivery
- `whatsapp:weissvanderpol` — DM to user (lead-scout only)
- `telegram:Fun4me-IA` — group delivery

NEVER use bare `whatsapp` or bare `telegram` — they error.

## Quick Diagnostic: "Site running but returning 000"

When `curl -so /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' https://domain.com` returns `000`:

1. **DNS check**: `dig +short domain.com` — empty means no A record in Cloudflare
2. **Docker check**: `docker service ls | grep service_name` — X/X replicas means it's running
3. **Traefik route check**: `docker service inspect svc_name --format '{{range $k,$v := .Spec.TaskTemplate.ContainerSpec.Labels}}{{$k}}={{$v}}{{"\n"}}{{end}}' | grep traefik` — empty labels = no route
4. **Fix DNS**: Add A record manually in Cloudflare dashboard (CF token is read-only)
5. **Fix labels**: Create compose file with labels → `docker stack deploy -c compose.yml`

Component usage: conditional classes with `transitionDelay` per index. ## Tailwind CSS v4: `@theme` Pattern (NOT v3 `@tailwind` directives) Tailwind v4 uses `@import "tailwindcss"` and `@theme {}` blocks — NOT the old `@tailwind base/components/utilities` directives. The installed PostCSS plugin `@tailwindcss/postcss` is v4-only. ### globals.css (v4 correct) ```css @import "tailwindcss"; @theme { --color-primary: #0F0F0F; --color-secondary: #FF6B35; --color-surface: #1A1A1A; --color-foreground: #F5F5F5; --color-background: #0A0A0A; --font-heading: "Playfair Display", serif; --font-body: "Inter", sans-serif; } body { font-family: var(--font-body); color: var(--color-foreground); background: var(--color-background); } ``` ### Migration from v3 to v4 - Remove `@tailwind base/components/utilities` and `tailwind.config.ts` - Remove `@tailwindcss/postcss` config from `postcss.config.mjs` — it's auto-detected - Add `@import "tailwindcss"` at top of `globals.css` - Add `@theme {}` block with `--color-*`, `--font-*`, `--radius-*` tokens - Color references become `text-foreground` (not `text-gray-900`), `bg-background` (not `bg-white`), `border-border` (not `border-gray-200`) - v3 `@apply` still works; v3 `@layer` directives are ignored (use plain CSS instead) ### `tailwind.config.ts` is dead in v4 Do NOT create or maintain `tailwind.config.ts`. v4 ignores it completely. All config lives in `globals.css` `@theme {}` and `@utility {}`. If you see a postcss build error referencing `./app/globals.css` + `./app/layout.tsx`, it means the CSS has v3 syntax (`@tailwind base`) that v4's PostCSS plugin can't parse. ### Common v4 Build Error ``` Error: Turbopack build failed with 1 errors: ./app/globals.css → ./app/layout.tsx Expected '' # Change to avoid f-string collision: f'' # dangerouslySetInnerHTML (quadruple braces needed) f'dangerouslySetInnerHTML={{{{ __html: JSON.stringify(jsonLd) }}}}' ``` ### Wrong — produces literal `${...}` in HTML ```python f'' # Produces: className="${montserrat.variable} ${playfair.variable}" ``` ### Rule of thumb Avoid JS template literals (`\`...${var}...\``) inside Python f-strings. Use string concatenation or className joining instead. For `dangerouslySetInnerHTML`, count braces: Python `{{}}` → JSX `{}` → inner `{{}}` → final `{{ __html: ... }}` → **need `{{{{ }}}}` in Python**. ## Service Auto-Generation Data Bug The auto-generated `services.tsx` references `cat.category` but the JSON data uses key `"name"`: ```typescript // WRONG — renders "undefined"

{cat.category}

// RIGHT const cats = [{ "name": "Memberships", items: [...] }]

{cat.name}

``` Always verify that the JSX accessor key matches the JSON object key when auto-generating component code. ## Client Type: Creative Agency / Portfolio Site For creative agencies, production houses, and portfolio-based businesses, use a DIFFERENT template than standard 10-page business sites: ### Key Design Differences | Feature | Standard Business | Agency | |---------|------------------|--------| | Theme | Light, white | Dark (near-black + accent color) | | Hero | Gradient or image | **Full-screen showreel video** | | Primary pages | Services, Contact, About | **Portfolio, Case Studies, Clients** | | Trust signals | Testimonials, years | **Client logos, work samples** | | Navigation | Simple links | **Filter + category system** | | Language | Spanish only | **ES + EN bilingual** | | Content type | Static text | **JSON-based portfolio data** | | CTAs | "Reservar", "Contactar" | "Ver Portfolio", "Proyecto similar" | | Standard pages | 10 | **15+ (10 case studies minimum)** | ### Dark Theme Palette ```css --color-primary: #0F0F0F; /* near-black */ --color-secondary: #FF6B35; /* energetic orange */ --color-surface: #1A1A1A; /* dark cards */ --color-surface-light: #2A2A2A; /* hover states */ --color-background: #0A0A0A; /* page background */ --color-foreground: #F5F5F5; /* light text */ ``` ### Portfolio Data Pattern Store projects as JSON files in `content/portfolio/`: ```json { "title": "Project Name", "client": "Client Name", "category": "video|photo|brand|events|marketing", "year": "2025", "description": "ES description", "approach": "How we tackled it", "results": "What was achieved" } ``` Portfolio pages use `generateStaticParams()` reading from the JSON directory for SSG. ### Minimum Page Set for Agencies - Home (showreel hero + featured work + client logos) - Portfolio (filterable grid with URL-based filter) - Portfolio/[slug] (case study with description, approach, results, gallery, "quiero algo similar" CTA) - Services (expandable service cards) - About (team + values + story) - Clients (logo wall + testimonials) - Blog + Blog/[slug] - Contact (form + WhatsApp + map) - FAQ - Legal pages ### i18n for Agencies (ES/EN) Many PY agencies serve international clients. Implement language toggle early: - Content in `content/es.json` and `content/en.json` - URL-based switching: `/en/portfolio`, `/en/contact` - Detect via `Accept-Language` header on first visit, persist in localStorage - Store as simple JSON key-value pairs, not next-intl (too heavy for small sites) ## Technical Pitfalls ### Service-at-Home Business Model For mobile beauty/wellness services (no fixed location, owner travels to client), see `references/service-at-home-business-model.md`. Key differences from e-commerce: session-based pricing, prep instructions, coverage zones, zero-cost marketing via owner's existing job (Uber). ### shadcn/ui duplicate keyframes `npx shadcn@latest add accordion` appends duplicate `keyframes`/`animation` in `tailwind.config.ts`. TypeScript fails: "multiple properties with same name." Fix: deduplicate immediately. ### Next.js 15 async params `params` are `Promise<{ slug: string }>`. Must `await params`: ```typescript export default async function Page({ params }: { params: Promise<{ slug: string }> }) { const { slug } = await params; } ``` ### HTML comments in JSX `` breaks JSX compilation silently. Use `{/* */}`. Watch for this in generated template code. ### `` placement in layout.tsx In Next.js App Router, `` must be INSIDE ``, not before it. Putting `` before `` causes webpack syntax errors. Prefer the `metadata` export for most head tags; use `` child of `` only for things like ``. ### Don't use fake names in placeholder reviews Use "Paciente Verificado" / "Verified Patient" — not fabricated names. ### Docker network mismatch `docker-compose.yml` network must match the actual VPS setup. If VPS uses Swarm (`agent-net` overlay), standalone containers need a bridge network + manual Traefik connection. ### Docker healthcheck IPv6 trap Alpine-based images resolve `localhost` to `::1` (IPv6), but Next.js standalone binds to `0.0.0.0` (IPv4 only). Use `127.0.0.1` explicitly: ```yaml healthcheck: test: ["CMD", "wget", "--spider", "http://127.0.0.1:3000/api/health"] # NOT localhost ``` Traefik v3 **skips unhealthy/starting containers** — if healthcheck fails, the route never appears. Debug with: `docker exec $(docker ps -q -f name=traefik) cat /var/log/traefik/traefik.log | grep "Filtering unhealthy"` ### Traefik Docker provider label for standalone containers When Traefik runs in Swarm mode (`--providers.swarm=true`) but also discovers standalone containers (`--providers.docker.endpoint`), you MUST add the `traefik.docker.network` label so Traefik knows which network to use: ```yaml labels: - "traefik.docker.network=myproject_mynet" # full compose-prefixed name ``` ## Phase 5: Post-Launch Fix Audit After the site is deployed and running, audit these common issues: ### Address/Location Completeness The `constants.ts` address object almost always ships with `street: ""`. Fix requires updating ALL locations that render address: - `lib/constants.ts` — source of truth - `components/layout/footer.tsx` — footer address display - `components/home/location.tsx` — homepage map section - `app/contacto/page.tsx` — contact page - `components/seo/json-ld.tsx` — structured data (Google) ### Google Maps Embed Coordinates Generic `!2d-57.58!3d-25.28` coordinates point to wrong location. Fix: 1. Geocode the actual address via Nominatim: `curl -s 'https://nominatim.openstreetmap.org/search?q=NEIGHBORHOOD+CITY+COUNTRY&format=json&limit=1' -H 'User-Agent: AppName/1.0'` 2. Update ALL iframe `src` attributes (search for `maps/embed` across codebase) 3. Update JSON-LD geo coordinates to match 4. Use `execute_code` with `search_files` to find ALL occurrences first ### Server Action Errors (Next.js 15) `[Error: Failed to find Server Action "x"]` in logs is from bots/crawlers sending invalid POST requests. This is a known Next.js 15 framework issue — NOT a code bug. Don't waste time investigating. ### Supabase Placeholder Pattern API routes (contact, newsletter, appointment) use try/catch around Supabase calls with `console.warn("Supabase not configured")`. This is correct — forms work (return success) even without a real Supabase project. Wiring real Supabase is a separate task requiring: create project → run migrations → update .env → rebuild. ### Quick Audit Commands ```bash # Find all address displays grep -rn "address\.\(city\|street\|country\)" components/ app/ --include="*.tsx" # Find all map embeds grep -rn "maps/embed\|maps.embed" --include="*.tsx" -l # Find all coordinate references grep -rn "latitude\|longitude\|-25\." --include="*.tsx" # Check for placeholder env vars grep "placeholder" .env # Check Docker logs for real errors (ignore Server Action noise) docker compose logs --tail=50 | grep -v "Server Action" ``` ## Adult Store / Sensitive Products Vertical (reference file) See `references/adult-store-ecommerce-patterns.md` for detailed patterns when building: - Adult stores (sex toys, lingerie, BDSM) - Any privacy-sensitive e-commerce - WhatsApp-first LATAM small business stores ### Critical Differences from Regular E-Commerce 1. **Privacy is the product**: Age gate, quick-exit, privacy mode, anonymous reviews, discreet packaging — these are feature requirements, not nice-to-haves 2. **WhatsApp is the checkout**: No cart, no payment gateway, no user accounts. Every "buy" button opens WhatsApp with prefilled message 3. **Content is educational, not titillating**: Beginner guides, safety info, material comparisons, care instructions 4. **SVGs replace product photos**: Until real photography is available, branded geometric SVGs with the site's color palette look professional instead of empty 5. **Safety badges build trust**: "Body Safe", "Hipoalergénico", "Impermeable" — these are conversion drivers 6. **Per-product FAQ**: Every product needs 2-3 Q&As (beginner-friendly, sound, cleaning, materials) 7. **Pricing: PYG only**: Dual-currency (PYG + USD) may be needed near borders but default to local currency ### Post-Build Checklist (add to Phase 3 Roast) 1. Promo carousel text spacing — no words smashed together 2. Blog content — full paragraphs, not truncated 3. Stats animation — starts from "—" not "0" 4. Age gate — timestamp with expiry (7d), not boolean 5. Footer — no duplicated social links (text + icon) 6. Blog categories — accent-normalized URL matching 7. Contact page — has hours, address, multiple touchpoints 8. Privacy/Terms — comprehensive (8+ sections each) 9. Product images — real SVGs or photos, not asterisk placeholders 10. Page titles — dynamic via useEffect 11. Breadcrumbs — visible (not just JSON-LD) 12. Accessibility — skip-to-content link 13. Price slider — single range, no overlapping handles 14. Anon review form — resets on submit, interactive stars 15. **Mobile product grids: Tailwind's `grid-cols-1` default is WRONG for card grids** — every product/card grid must explicitly set `grid-cols-2` as base class. Pattern: - Wrong: `className="grid gap-6 sm:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-3"` → 1 col on mobile - Right: `className="grid grid-cols-2 gap-6 sm:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-3"` → 2 cols on mobile - Applies to featured products, blog index, promotions, envio points, testimonials, skeleton loader, and any grid of cards - Also applies to `grid gap-6 sm:grid-cols-2` (no lg variant) — same fix: add `grid-cols-2` base - `search_files(pattern='grid gap-6 sm:grid-cols-2', ...)` finds all offenders ## Key Principles 1. **Don't skip Phase 0** — client photos/content are always the bottleneck 2. **Claim Google Business Profile FIRST** — generates leads before website exists 3. **WhatsApp is mandatory for LATAM** 4. **MVP must go live in 2 weeks max** — perfectionism kills momentum 5. **Blog content can be AI-assisted** but must be client-reviewed 6. **Don't overbuild booking** — in LATAM, WhatsApp confirmation is the norm 7. **Professional photography > stock photos** for local businesses 8. **SEO takes months** — set expectations; GBP is the quick win 9. **Build pages first, wire data later** — proven most efficient approach 10. **Close process/config epics as you go** — directory submissions, ad account setup, social media creation are tasks for the client, not code. Close them with notes. 11. **Admin shells count** — building the empty UI (tables, filters, KPI cards) with "Conectar Supabase" placeholders is legitimate progress. Wiring real data is a separate epic. 12. **Interactive tools drive leads** — risk assessment quizzes, symptom checkers, financing calculators, treatment comparisons all capture leads AND build SEO authority. Build these early. 13. **NPS-gated review generation** — Score ≥7 sends to Google Reviews; Score <7 captures internal feedback. Protects reputation while generating reviews. ## Supabase CMS Schema Template Standard tables for a business website: - `services` — title, slug, description, icon, image, sort_order - `team_members` — name, title, bio, photo, credentials - `testimonials` — patient_name, rating, text, source, is_featured - `gallery_cases` — title, before_image, after_image, category - `blog_posts` — slug, title, content, author, published_at - `contact_submissions` — name, phone, email, reason, message - `faq_items` — question, answer, category, sort_order - `appointment_requests` — name, phone, service, date, time, status - `settings` — key/value store for business hours, contact info - `patient_intake` — personal, medical, dental history, insurance - `newsletter_subscribers` — email, confirmed, active - `referral_codes` — patient, code, uses - `treatment_prices` — service, min/max price, currency - `promotions` — title, prices, badge, date range - `events` — title, date, venue, capacity, ticket_price, description (for event ticketing) Always add: RLS policies (public read content, public insert forms), updated_at triggers, seed data. ## Event Ticketing Integration When a client site needs event ticket sales with QR scanning at the door, see `references/event-ticketing-integration-options.md`. For non-WordPress sites, the recommended path is **pretix** (self-hosted or hosted) with the pretixSCAN mobile app — deployable on the existing VPS alongside client websites. ## Session Timeout Pattern Long autonomous sessions may timeout. The codebase persists. On resume: 1. `git log --oneline -5` — see last commit 2. `find app -name "page.tsx" | wc -l` — page count 3. `gh issue list --state open --json number -q 'length'` — remaining work 4. {"action": "write_file", "file_path": "references/client-asset-ingestion-gdrive.md", "name": "client-website-planning", "file_content": "# Google Drive Asset Ingestion for Client Sites

When a client shares a Google Drive folder (logos, achievements, photos, brand assets), use this pattern to download, classify, organize, and integrate into the repo.

## Tools

- `python3 -m gdown --folder "URL" -O /tmp/dest` — downloads entire folder
- `PIL.Image.open(f).size` — get dimensions for classification
- `cp` files into `public/client-name/assets-category/`

## Workflow

### 1. Download
```bash
python3 -m gdown --folder "https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/FOLDER_ID" -O /tmp/asset-folder
```

If `gdown` not installed: `pip3 install gdown`.

### 2. Classify by dimensions + naming patterns

Run this to auto-classify:

```python
from PIL import Image
import os

path = '/tmp/asset-folder'
for f in sorted(os.listdir(path)):
    if not (f.endswith('.jpg') or f.endswith('.png')):
        continue
    img = Image.open(f'{path}/{f}')
    w, h = img.size
    ratio = w / h
    size_kb = os.path.getsize(f'{path}/{f}') // 1024

    if f.startswith('Screenshot') and w == 1080 and h == 2400:
        cat = 'PHONE-SCREENSHOT'  # Amazon KDP / IG dashboard scroll
    elif '_n.jpg' in f:
        cat = 'INSTAGRAM-POST'    # 1080x1350 or square
    elif 300 < w < 900 and 300 < h < 900:
        if ratio > 1.4:         cat = 'BADGE-WIDE'
        elif ratio > 1.0:       cat = 'BADGE-LANDSCAPE'
        elif ratio > 0.85:      cat = 'BADGE-SQUARISH'
        else:                    cat = 'BADGE-PORTRAIT'
    else:
        cat = 'OTHER'

    print(f"{f:<55} {w}x{h:<10} {size_kb}KB  {cat}")
```

### 3. Classification rules of thumb

| Pattern | Likely content | Typical dims |
|---------|---------------|-------------|
| `Screenshot_*.png` 1080x2400 | Phone scroll — KDP dashboard, IG profile | 1080×2400 |
| `*_n.jpg` 1080×1350 | Instagram portrait post | 1080×1350 |
| UUID-named JPG 300-900px | Cropped achievement badge, ranking card, certificate | 400-800px |
| UUID-named WIDE ratio >1.4 | Ranking bar, badge row | 760×~500 |
| UUID-named SQUARE ~1.0 | Profile badge, selection card | 790×790 |

### 4. Copy to repo

```bash
mkdir -p public/client-name/assets-category/
cp /tmp/asset-folder/* public/client-name/assets-category/
```

### 5. Convert formats for web

`.jfif` files don't render in browsers — convert to `.png`:

```python
from PIL import Image
for f in os.listdir('path'):
    if f.endswith('.jfif'):
        img = Image.open(f)
        img.save(f.replace('.jfif', '.png'), 'PNG')
```

### 6. Build gallery page

Pattern for `/logros`-style gallery with lightbox:

- 3 sections: Badges/Grid, Screenshots, Social Media posts
- Click-to-zoom lightbox overlay
- Stats summary bar above the grid
- `loading="lazy"` on all images

## Pitfalls

- **Vision API may be down** — don't rely on it. Classify by dims + naming.
- **JFIF files** — Chrome/Firefox don't render them. Convert to PNG.
- **Large files** — screenshots can be 900KB+. OK for a gallery page but don't duplicate across pages.
- **UUID filenames** — unhelpful but keep them. Image alt text can be descriptive labels in a JS map.
- **`gdown --folder` auth** — public shared links work. No Google auth needed.
"}
